_meta.disclaimer: These controls are constructed for educational purposes to illustrate typical technology risk management patterns under SC-GL/6-2023. They are not verbatim extracts from the Securities Commission Malaysia guidelines. Always consult the official SC guidelines and qualified regulatory counsel.
_meta.alignment: SC-GL/6-2023 — Guidelines on Technology Risk Management

controls.name: Technology Risk Governance Framework
controls.description: Establish a comprehensive technology risk governance framework with clear roles, responsibilities, and reporting lines from board level through operational management for capital market entities.
keyActivities: Define board-level technology risk oversight responsibilities
Establish technology risk committee with cross-functional representation
Define technology risk appetite aligned with business strategy and SC expectations
Implement three lines of defence model for technology risk management

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc governance with no formal framework
maturity.mature: Documented framework with committee structure
maturity.advanced: Integrated governance with real-time risk dashboards

controls.name: CISO Designation & Authority
controls.description: Designate a Chief Information Security Officer or equivalent with sufficient authority, independence, and resources to manage technology and cyber security risks across the capital market entity.
keyActivities: Appoint a qualified CISO with direct reporting line to senior management or board
Define CISO authority, scope, and resource allocation
Ensure CISO independence from technology operations functions
Establish regular CISO reporting to board or board committee

maturity.basic: Security responsibilities assigned to IT manager
maturity.mature: Dedicated CISO with board reporting
maturity.advanced: CISO with enterprise-wide authority and strategic influence

controls.name: Technology Risk Policy Suite
controls.description: Develop and maintain a comprehensive suite of technology risk management policies covering all key domains, reviewed and approved by the board at least annually.
keyActivities: Develop policies covering cybersecurity, data management, BCP, cloud, outsourcing, and incident management
Ensure policies reference SC-GL/6-2023 requirements
Obtain board approval for all technology risk policies
Communicate policies to all relevant personnel and stakeholders

maturity.basic: Partial policy coverage with inconsistent review
maturity.mature: Complete policy suite with annual board review
maturity.advanced: Dynamic policies with automated compliance monitoring

controls.name: Technology Risk Reporting
controls.description: Establish regular technology risk reporting to the board and senior management, including risk posture, incident trends, compliance status, and emerging technology risks.
keyActivities: Produce quarterly technology risk reports for board and senior management
Include key risk indicators, incident trends, and compliance metrics
Report on emerging technology risks and their potential impact
Track and report on remediation of technology audit findings

maturity.mature: Structured quarterly reporting with KRIs
maturity.advanced: Real-time risk dashboard with predictive analytics

controls.name: Cyber Threat Management
controls.description: Implement a structured approach to identifying, assessing, and responding to cyber threats targeting capital market systems, including threat intelligence consumption and threat hunting capabilities.
keyActivities: Establish threat intelligence programme with industry and government feeds
Conduct periodic threat assessments for capital market-specific threats
Implement threat hunting capabilities for advanced persistent threats
Participate in SC and industry threat information sharing

maturity.mature: Proactive threat intelligence with SOC
maturity.advanced: Advanced threat hunting with ML-based detection

controls.name: Security Operations Centre
controls.description: Establish and maintain security monitoring capabilities for continuous surveillance of capital market systems, with 24/7 coverage for critical market infrastructure.
keyActivities: Deploy SIEM for centralised log collection and correlation
Establish 24/7 monitoring for critical trading and settlement systems
Define and tune detection rules for capital market-specific attack patterns
Conduct regular SOC effectiveness assessments

maturity.basic: Basic log monitoring during business hours
maturity.mature: 24/7 SOC with SIEM and SOAR
maturity.advanced: AI-augmented SOC with automated response orchestration

controls.name: Vulnerability Management Programme
controls.description: Implement a systematic vulnerability identification, assessment, and remediation programme for all capital market technology systems with defined SLAs for patch deployment.
keyActivities: Conduct regular vulnerability scans of all technology assets
Perform annual penetration testing of critical market systems
Define risk-based patch management SLAs
Track vulnerability remediation to closure with exception management

maturity.mature: Regular scanning with risk-based patching SLAs
maturity.advanced: Continuous scanning with automated patch deployment

controls.name: Network Security Architecture
controls.description: Design and maintain a secure network architecture for capital market systems with appropriate segmentation, access controls, and protection of trading and market data networks.
keyActivities: Implement network segmentation between trading, settlement, and corporate networks
Deploy intrusion detection and prevention systems for market infrastructure
Secure market data feeds and connectivity with participants
Review network architecture annually against evolving threat landscape

maturity.mature: Segmented network with IDS/IPS and monitoring
maturity.advanced: Zero-trust architecture with microsegmentation

controls.name: Technology Risk Assessment Framework
controls.description: Establish a systematic framework for identifying, assessing, and treating technology risks specific to capital market operations, aligned with the entity's risk appetite and SC expectations.
keyActivities: Define technology risk assessment methodology aligned with SC-GL/6-2023
Conduct annual comprehensive technology risk assessment
Assess risks from algorithmic trading systems and market infrastructure dependencies
Integrate technology risk assessment with enterprise risk management

maturity.mature: Structured annual assessment with defined methodology
maturity.advanced: Continuous risk assessment with automated risk signals

controls.name: Algorithmic Trading Risk Controls
controls.description: Implement pre-trade and post-trade risk controls for algorithmic and automated trading systems, including kill switches, position limits, and market manipulation prevention measures.
keyActivities: Implement pre-trade risk checks (price limits, order size limits, position limits)
Deploy kill switch capability for immediate halt of algorithmic trading
Conduct algorithm testing and approval process before production deployment
Monitor for market manipulation patterns in algorithmic trading activity

maturity.mature: Comprehensive pre-trade and post-trade controls with kill switch
maturity.advanced: Real-time ML-based anomaly detection with automated circuit breakers

controls.name: Technology Change Risk Assessment
controls.description: Assess technology risks associated with system changes, upgrades, and new technology deployments before implementation, with approval gates proportionate to change risk level.
keyActivities: Classify changes by risk level (critical, significant, standard)
Conduct risk assessment for all critical and significant changes
Implement testing and approval gates before production deployment
Define rollback procedures for failed changes

maturity.mature: Risk-based change classification with approval gates
maturity.advanced: Automated risk scoring with continuous deployment pipelines

controls.name: Cloud Risk Assessment
controls.description: Conduct cloud-specific risk assessments for all proposed cloud adoptions, covering shared responsibility, data sovereignty, and capital market-specific considerations for client information and market data.
keyActivities: Assess cloud-specific risks including shared responsibility and multi-tenancy
Evaluate data residency implications for client information and market data
Review CSP certifications and independent assurance reports
Document shared responsibility matrix for each cloud engagement

maturity.mature: Structured cloud risk framework applied per engagement
maturity.advanced: Continuous cloud security posture monitoring

controls.name: Cloud Data Governance
controls.description: Implement data governance controls for cloud-hosted capital market data, including classification, encryption, access controls, and data residency compliance for client and market information.
keyActivities: Classify all data types and apply appropriate protection in cloud environments
Implement encryption with customer-managed keys for sensitive data
Enforce data residency requirements for client information
Monitor and audit data access in cloud environments

maturity.mature: BYOK with data classification and access monitoring
maturity.advanced: Automated data governance with DLP and rights management

controls.name: Cloud Exit Strategy
controls.description: Develop and maintain exit strategies for cloud services to ensure data portability and continuity of capital market operations in the event of CSP failure or contract termination.
keyActivities: Document exit plans for all material cloud deployments
Design cloud workloads for portability using open standards
Test data extraction and migration procedures periodically
Identify alternative cloud providers or on-premises fallback options

maturity.mature: Documented exit plans with portability requirements
maturity.advanced: Tested multi-cloud failover with automated migration

controls.name: Third-Party Risk Assessment
controls.description: Conduct risk assessments for all third-party technology service providers, with depth proportionate to the criticality of services and access to client data or market systems.
keyActivities: Classify third parties by risk tier based on service criticality
Conduct comprehensive due diligence for critical service providers
Assess vendor security posture through questionnaires and independent reviews
Monitor vendor financial health and operational stability

maturity.mature: Tiered assessment programme with annual reviews
maturity.advanced: Continuous vendor risk monitoring with automated scoring

controls.name: Outsourcing Governance
controls.description: Establish governance structures for technology outsourcing arrangements aligned with SC-GL/6-2023 requirements, including board oversight, contractual standards, and monitoring mechanisms.
keyActivities: Obtain board approval for material outsourcing arrangements
Maintain outsourcing policy aligned with SC-GL/6-2023
Include mandatory contractual provisions (SLA, right-to-audit, data protection)
Report outsourcing risk status to board quarterly

maturity.mature: Formal governance with policy and board oversight
maturity.advanced: Integrated outsourcing governance with enterprise risk

controls.name: Vendor Performance Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor service provider performance against agreed service levels and contractual obligations, with escalation procedures for persistent underperformance.
keyActivities: Monitor SLA compliance for all outsourced technology services
Generate monthly performance reports for critical vendors
Implement escalation procedures for SLA breaches
Conduct periodic vendor satisfaction and relationship reviews

maturity.mature: Automated SLA monitoring with exception reporting
maturity.advanced: Predictive performance analytics with proactive management

controls.name: Fourth-Party Oversight
controls.description: Identify and manage risks from service providers' sub-contractors that may impact capital market operations or access client data and market information.
keyActivities: Require disclosure of sub-contracting for critical services
Assess fourth-party risks for critical supply chains
Include sub-contractor oversight provisions in contracts
Monitor supply chain changes and concentration risks

maturity.mature: Fourth-party register with assessment
maturity.advanced: Continuous supply chain risk monitoring

controls.name: Data Governance Framework
controls.description: Establish a comprehensive data governance framework for capital market data, including market data integrity, client information protection, and data quality management.
keyActivities: Define data governance roles, responsibilities, and accountability
Implement data classification scheme for market and client data
Establish data quality standards and monitoring
Ensure compliance with PDPA and SC data protection requirements

maturity.mature: Documented framework with classification and quality controls
maturity.advanced: Automated data governance with lineage tracking

controls.name: Market Data Integrity
controls.description: Implement controls to ensure the integrity, accuracy, and timeliness of market data used in trading, settlement, and reporting operations.
keyActivities: Implement data validation checks for market data feeds
Monitor market data latency and accuracy metrics
Establish reconciliation processes for critical market data
Define data correction and restatement procedures

maturity.mature: Automated validation with reconciliation
maturity.advanced: Real-time data quality monitoring with anomaly detection

controls.name: Client Data Protection
controls.description: Implement data protection controls for client information held by capital market intermediaries, including encryption, access controls, and data loss prevention.
keyActivities: Encrypt client data at rest and in transit
Implement role-based access controls for client information systems
Deploy data loss prevention tools for client data
Conduct regular data protection impact assessments

maturity.basic: Basic encryption with minimal access controls
maturity.mature: Comprehensive DLP with RBAC and monitoring
maturity.advanced: Zero-trust data protection with automated classification

controls.name: BCM Programme for Market Operations
controls.description: Establish a business continuity management programme specific to capital market operations, ensuring trading, clearing, and settlement services can continue during disruptions.
keyActivities: Conduct business impact analysis for all capital market functions
Develop BCP covering trading, clearing, settlement, and custody operations
Define RTO and RPO for market infrastructure components
Coordinate BCP with market operators and clearing houses

maturity.mature: Comprehensive BCP with market operation focus
maturity.advanced: Dynamic BCP with real-time impact assessment

controls.name: Trading System Resilience
controls.description: Ensure trading systems have sufficient resilience, redundancy, and failover capabilities to maintain market access and order execution during technology disruptions.
keyActivities: Implement redundant trading infrastructure with automatic failover
Define capacity requirements with headroom for market volatility
Test trading system failover procedures regularly
Establish alternative trading channels for primary system failures

maturity.basic: Single trading system with manual recovery
maturity.mature: Redundant systems with tested failover
maturity.advanced: Active-active trading with zero-downtime failover

controls.name: DR Testing Programme
controls.description: Conduct regular disaster recovery testing for capital market systems, including participation in industry-wide DR exercises coordinated by Bursa Malaysia or SC.
keyActivities: Conduct annual DR testing for all critical market systems
Participate in industry-wide DR exercises as required by SC or Bursa
Test data recovery and system restoration procedures
Document test results and remediate gaps

maturity.mature: Annual DR testing with industry participation
maturity.advanced: Continuous DR readiness with automated failover testing

controls.name: Settlement Continuity
controls.description: Ensure settlement processes can continue during technology disruptions, with alternative settlement procedures and coordination with central depositories and clearing houses.
keyActivities: Define alternative settlement procedures for system failures
Coordinate settlement continuity with Bursa Malaysia Depository
Test manual settlement fallback procedures
Maintain communication protocols with clearing and depository participants

maturity.mature: Documented manual fallback with testing
maturity.advanced: Automated settlement failover with real-time coordination

controls.name: Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Develop and maintain a technology incident response plan specific to capital market operations, including classification, escalation, containment, and recovery procedures.
keyActivities: Define incident classification and severity levels for market operations
Establish incident response team with clear roles and responsibilities
Define escalation procedures including SC notification requirements
Conduct tabletop exercises and incident simulations

maturity.mature: Documented plan with regular exercises
maturity.advanced: Automated incident orchestration with playbooks

controls.name: SC Incident Notification
controls.description: Ensure timely notification to the Securities Commission Malaysia of significant technology incidents affecting capital market operations, as required by SC-GL/6-2023.
keyActivities: Define notification criteria and thresholds for SC reporting
Establish notification workflow with defined timelines
Prepare incident notification templates with required information
Track SC responses and address any conditions

maturity.mature: Standardised notification process with tracking
maturity.advanced: Automated notification workflow with regulatory alerts

controls.name: Cyber Incident Coordination
controls.description: Coordinate cyber incident response with market participants, SC, and other relevant authorities to contain and mitigate the impact of incidents on capital market operations.
keyActivities: Establish communication protocols with market participants and regulators
Participate in industry incident response coordination mechanisms
Share threat intelligence with SC and peer institutions
Conduct post-incident reviews with coordination partners

maturity.mature: Established protocols with key stakeholders
maturity.advanced: Real-time coordination platform with automated sharing

controls.name: Incident Post-Mortem & Lessons Learned
controls.description: Conduct structured post-incident reviews to identify root causes, lessons learned, and improvement actions to prevent recurrence of technology incidents.
keyActivities: Conduct post-mortem review within defined timeframe after incident closure
Identify root causes and contributing factors
Document lessons learned and improvement actions
Track implementation of improvement actions to closure

maturity.mature: Structured post-mortem with tracked actions
maturity.advanced: Automated trend analysis with preventive recommendations

controls.name: Technology Audit Programme
controls.description: Establish a risk-based technology audit programme that covers all key technology risk domains and aligns with SC regulatory expectations for independent assurance.
keyActivities: Develop risk-based technology audit plan covering all domains
Engage qualified technology auditors with capital market experience
Report technology audit findings to board audit committee
Track remediation of audit findings to closure

maturity.mature: Risk-based annual programme with qualified auditors
maturity.advanced: Continuous assurance with automated testing

controls.name: Regulatory Examination Readiness
controls.description: Maintain readiness for SC regulatory examinations of technology risk management, including evidence management, documentation, and rapid response capabilities.
keyActivities: Maintain current evidence for all SC-GL/6-2023 requirements
Conduct periodic self-assessments against SC guidelines
Prepare examination response team and protocols
Track and address prior examination findings

maturity.mature: Standing evidence library with regular updates
maturity.advanced: Continuous compliance monitoring with regulatory dashboard

controls.name: Penetration Testing Programme
controls.description: Conduct regular penetration testing of capital market systems by qualified independent testers, with scope covering trading systems, client portals, and market connectivity.
keyActivities: Engage qualified independent penetration testers annually
Define scope covering critical market systems and client-facing applications
Remediate critical and high findings within defined SLAs
Report penetration testing results to board audit committee

maturity.mature: Annual testing with qualified testers
maturity.advanced: Continuous red team exercises with purple team collaboration

controls.name: Algorithmic Trading Governance
controls.description: Establish governance framework for algorithmic and automated trading systems, including approval processes, testing requirements, and ongoing monitoring.
keyActivities: Establish approval process for new algorithms before production deployment
Define testing requirements including backtesting and simulation
Monitor algorithm performance and risk metrics in production
Review and re-approve algorithms periodically

maturity.mature: Documented approval and testing process
maturity.advanced: Automated algorithm monitoring with anomaly detection

controls.name: AI/ML Risk Management
controls.description: Assess and manage risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning in capital market operations, including model risk, bias, and explainability.
keyActivities: Conduct model risk assessment for AI/ML applications in trading and compliance
Implement model validation and testing procedures
Assess and mitigate algorithmic bias in client-facing applications
Establish explainability requirements for AI/ML-driven decisions

maturity.mature: Model risk management for AI applications
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive AI governance with continuous monitoring

controls.name: DLT & Digital Asset Controls
controls.description: Implement risk management controls for distributed ledger technology and digital asset activities, including smart contract security, wallet management, and regulatory compliance.
keyActivities: Conduct security assessments for smart contracts before deployment
Implement secure key management and wallet controls for digital assets
Ensure regulatory compliance for digital asset activities under SC guidelines
Monitor DLT infrastructure for security and performance

maturity.mature: Security assessments and key management procedures
maturity.advanced: Formal verification of smart contracts with continuous monitoring
